title: bermudiana bermudiana var. exaltata
intro: Bermudiana bermudiana var. exaltata, also known as the sea lavender or rosemary of the sea, is a subspecies of the Bermudiana bermudiana plant. This plant is a type of flowering shrub that is native to the coastal regions of the Mediterranean and Atlantic coasts. The Bermudiana bermudiana var. exaltata has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to promote digestion, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
